action.reasoning: Observation: short multitask PPO training completed in ~178s and produced checkpoints, but eval was skipped because the local smoke test called predict.py with /data/eval_config.json, which does not exist in this container root. Analysis: the core pipeline likely works; the immediate blocker is predict.py robustness and local test path handling. Also current predict.py resets VecEnv awkwardly and uses normalized rewards from VecNormalize during evaluation, which would make reported cumulative rewards incorrect for server-side success recomputation. Decision: fix predict.py to (1) support either <data_path>/eval_config.json or a fallback search, (2) evaluate on raw env rewards while still normalizing observations via loaded VecNormalize stats, and (3) simplify deterministic rollout with plain gym envs. Also update train.py minimally to save metadata cleanly. Then run a local smoke test against the actual eval_config location if present and submit eval with the existing checkpoint to measure baseline score before longer training.
action.command: if [ -f /data/humanoidbench_test/eval_config.json ]; then python predict.py --data_path /data/humanoidbench_test --checkpoint ckpt/baseline --output baseline_pred.json; elif [ -f /data/eval_config.json ]; then python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint ckpt/baseline --output baseline_pred.json; else echo 'No local eval_config found; skipping smoke rollout'; fi && ls ckpt/baseline && test -f ckpt/baseline/final_model.zip
